title: geijera parviflora var. crassifolia
intro: Geijera parviflora var. crassifolia, also known as the small-leaf geijera or crassifolia, is a plant species native to Australia. It is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the Rutaceae family, commonly known as citrus or rue family. Geijera parviflora var. crassifolia has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, and its medicinal properties include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, and can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions to reap its health benefits.
